programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Trad housewife Charlie is hooked to the heavily tattooed Rob. And can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Saras rather chatty nature?

programme.title: Millionaire Hoarders
programme.desc: Retired artists Carole and Roger have crammed their nine-bed home with objects from around the world. Will they part with possessions to raise much-needed cash?

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Desiree Burch joins Joe Lycett for some snow-filled shenanigans in Lapland, where they have a crack at ice fishing, get carried away on a sleigh-ride and spark hope for some aurorae gazing.

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows us how to create beautiful crafts with items we already own. She transforms some fabric by dyeing it with turmeric, and embellishes an old lampshade.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, host Dean Faulkner, a dog and cattery owner, is keen to impress with his cockney knees-up themed dinner party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridge its the turn of Kay Greenwood to make dinner, with tempura prawns and beef Stroganoff on the menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridge, Matt Suillivans home-grown English menu, including posh fish and chips and rice pudding, isnt quite what the guests are expecting.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day four in Cambridge and will Hawa Sydiques adventurous menu cause her more problems than its worth? Could a 20-piece samba band in her front room prove a bit much?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night of the competition in Cambridge, and proud Scouser David Goodier is determined to show his southern rivals how to throw a winning dinner party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bradford. First host Kulvir fuses Punjabi food with British classics. But could a dessert downfall scupper his supper?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jason plans a menu of Yorkshires finest but keeps throwing his efforts in the bin! After some postcode palaver, have Jasons chances of winning the grand bin and gone?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gabby hopes that her Sardinian soiree and Dolce Vita dinner will sweeten the deal. And after revealing her nickname on night one, will her guests get a right mouthful?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Akili aims to enchant her guests with exotic elegance and a fusion of flavours. Can she deliver a prize-worthy party or will her ice cream leave her out in the cold?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The weeks last host Tom draws on his French heritage to deliver simple dishes executed well. And as the final scores are revealed, who has the je ne sais quois?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth visits Eleanor and Chris at their stunning cliff-top apartment in Tenerife. Danni Menzies sees if Peter and Karen are living the off-grid life they craved in Tarragona.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Geraldine and Tim are looking for a spacious holiday home that their whole family can enjoy on Spains glorious Costa Tropical. Sara Damergi shows them what £180k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps sisters Sheila and Jean look for a holiday home on Spains Mijas Costa for all their family and friends to enjoy for £120k. Will they find their dream place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Its full steam ahead for train driver Wayne and train manager Kate, as Lee Juggurnauth shows them the best of what their £85k budget can get them in the town of Pinar de Campoverde, Spain.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Antonia and Ross love the beautiful island of Ibiza. Having holidayed there many times, they now want their own holiday home. Can Lucy Alexander deliver their dream?

programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Sarah and Andy find the perfect place for their whole family, including their beloved dog, Ruby, on Spains southern Costa Blanca, for their budget of £160k.

programme.desc: Rookie interceptor Rachael faces a baptism of fire including a chase with a pregnant passenger, and a deluded motorist calls the police to report interceptor Chris for stealing his car.

programme.desc: AJ and Richie are in pursuit of a van speeding through narrow country lanes. Jacko and the team box in a van carrying large quantities of cannabis. Plus, sparks fly when dog handler Justin and police dog Elsa capture two burglars on a building site.

programme.desc: Traffic chaos in the Tyne Tunnel, and shocking driving as a car reverses down a slip road with an unlicensed driver at the wheel who hasnt even taken his test.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: On the hunt for a van linked to drug dealing and organised crime in Sunderland, and a misted-up car in Newcastle draws more attention to itself with a V-sign.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders

programme.title: Cold Pursuit
programme.desc: Nels Coxmans quiet life as a snowplow driver comes crashing down when his beloved son dies under mysterious circumstances. His search for the truth soon becomes a quest for revenge against a psychotic drug lord named Viking and his sleazy henchmen. Transformed from upstanding citizen to coldblooded vigilante, Coxman unwittingly sets off a chain of events that includes a kidnapping, a series of deadly misunderstandings and a turf war between Viking and a rival boss.

programme.title: Miss Potter
programme.desc: Based on the life of early 20th-century author Beatrix Potter, creator of Peter Rabbit. As a young woman Potter rails against her parents wishes for her to marry and settle down. Her early attempts to find a publisher for her childrens stories are unsuccessful, but an offer from a small firm will turn her into a literary phenomenon.

programme.desc: A massive manhunt ensues after three suspects flee onto a farm, the team raids a drugs den and officers stumble across a suspicious-looking postman.

An geama beò à Phàirce Hampden le Calum MacAmhlaidh agus Ailig OHenley.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a leafy suburb of Hampshire, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits an unrivalled collection of Matchbox toy cars dating back to the 1950s. Collector Nigel Cooper reckons he has about 25,000 miniature motors, some he bought as a boy with his pocket money. Among his rarest models is a coronation coach and horses from 1953 with a surprising story. In neighbouring Surrey, two teddy-bear-loving teams compete for profits at a huge toy fair at Sandown Park Racecourse.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley celebrates International Womens Day with stories of inspiring women of faith, past and present. In St Pauls Cathedral, Kate meets the most Rev Dame Sarah Mullally ahead of her installation as the first female archbishop of Canterbury in history. Archbishop Sarah Mullally talks about her journey of faith, what her previous vocation as a nurse brings to her new role and how far the Church of England has come since 1994 when women were first ordained.

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.sub-title: Sugar and Spice: A British Pudding Adventure
programme.desc: Baker and dessert-lover Briony May Williams embarks on a journey to discover whether warnings of the imminent demise of the British pudding are overcooked. She meets a pudding fan club in the Cotswolds, heads to the Lake District to meet the makers of what may have become the countrys favourite pudding and tests out the sweet tooths of pudding fans of the future to see if theres any hope for this British classic.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a leafy suburb of Hampshire, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits an unrivalled collection of Matchbox toy cars dating back to the 1950s. Collector Nigel Cooper reckons he has about 25,000 miniature motors, some he bought as a boy with his pocket money. Among his rarest models is a coronation coach and horses from 1953 with a surprising story. In neighbouring Surrey, two teddy-bear-loving teams compete for profits at a huge toy fair at Sandown Park Racecourse.

programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley celebrates International Womens Day with stories of inspiring women of faith, past and present. In St Pauls Cathedral, Kate meets the most Rev Dame Sarah Mullally ahead of her installation as the first female archbishop of Canterbury in history. Archbishop Sarah Mullally talks about her journey of faith, what her previous vocation as a nurse brings to her new role and how far the Church of England has come since 1994 when women were first ordained.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple currently living in Manchester are hoping to up sticks to the Cheshire countryside with their young daughter. They have requested a four bedroom, detached family home with a large, private garden, and Nicki Chapman has a very tempting selection of properties that suit the brief, as well as a mystery house that makes them reconsider their wish list.

programme.title: Spider-Man 3
programme.desc: Life is sweet for Peter Parker, MJ loves him, her career is taking off and Spidey-mania is sweeping New York. However, when an alien parasite bonds to his suit, its not long before the good times and Spidey turn bad. While his ugly ego is taking centre stage, MJ is dumped from her Broadway show, Harry remembers Spider-Mans role in his fathers death, and Peter is consumed by revenge.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Twelve months after exposing Correction in the most dramatic circumstances, whistleblower Rachel Carey is back, ready to launch Operation Veritas, a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing. That is until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness.

programme.desc: Linda makes a bad situation worse, Julie faces a moral dilemma, and Jack fears Ravi isnt up to the job.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is conflicted over his loyalties, Max takes action on Lindas behalf, and Howie is determined to make a difference.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark walks into a risky situation, Kim gets a surprise at the Albert, and theres conflict between Nigels loved ones.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil prepares for a big change. Lexi gives some sage advice.

programme.title: The End We Start From
programme.desc: When catastrophic floods submerge London, a new mother embarks on a dangerous journey to find a safe refuge, all the while trying to protect her baby from the surrounding chaos.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Award-winning comedian Jayde Adams is on hand to help as for one night only the queens form comedy double acts for RuPauls Fringe Festival. But who will have the last laugh? Amandaland star Lucy Punch joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton as another queen sashays away.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After being invited to a bar crawl on Khaosan Road, Zara gets under the skin of Bangkoks notorious red light district, Soi Cowboy - but filming is swiftly shut down by local bar owners and the police. When Zara meets Mike Yu, a young British expat living a life of luxury in the Thai capital, she realises theres more to the city than meets the eye. Meanwhile, a visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law in Thailand.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: In Asias sex capital, Pattaya, Zara meets British men chasing cheap thrills, before heading to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After a wild and crazy night at Ko Phangans Full Moon Party, Zara travels to Phuket and experiences a taste of Thailands luxury lifestyle.

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy retraces Sarms last known movements before her disappearance from a charter yacht in 2021. She hears Sarms boyfriend, Ryan Bane, give his account of what happened through his lawyer.

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Tir meets with island police and is given CCTV footage that has never been released, showing Sarms last known movements with her boyfriend, Ryan Bane.

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Tir searches for the yacht Sarm vanished from and tracks down Ryan Banes ex-wife, hearing first-hand about her experiences while married to him.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy faces his comeback fight in Budapest, and the pressure is on. How will he cope after nearly two years out of the ring?

programme.title: The Great Caruso

programme.title: Mary Berry - Love to Cook
programme.desc: Mary shows us recipes that will build confidence in the kitchen, including an easy stir-fry hoisin chicken, a mouthwatering salmon and dill burger that even the most inexperienced cooks can master.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Scotland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Scotland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today Flog It! comes from the beautiful St Albans Cathedral and Abbey in Hertfordshire. Antique experts Claire Rawle and James Lewis are on hand to value antiques and collectibles and to take them off to auction.

programme.title: The Bridge at Remagen
programme.desc: World War II film chronicling the tense battle to hold the Ludendorff Bridge at Remagen, the only bridge still standing across the Rhine towards the end of the war. The German officer in charge of the region faces a dilemma - destroy the bridge and strand 50,000 of his own troops on the wrong side of the river, or leave the bridge intact and effectively give up the Fatherland to the advancing Allied forces.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Gladiator Apollo on their knowledge of the UK. Todays tour is of Derry/Londonderry.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Mountain Lions: Big Cats in High Places
programme.desc: Set in Wyomings spectacular Rocky Mountains, the dramatic story of two mountain lion mothers struggling to raise their kittens is helping scientists rewrite our understanding of these elusive predators.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters and disability rights campaigners Katie Piper and Adam Pearson attempt to turn £400 into a larger sum by flipping antiques for profit. Katie and Adam rummage in a southerly direction from Northampton to the River Thames.

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya visits Italy and Germany to explore how theyre changing in the turbulent new world. She attends the Palio in Siena, joins a Nato exercise and meets a family building a nuclear bunker.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some indelible hits by artists we hardly heard from again. Featuring Peter Sarstedts Where Do You Go To My Lovely? a number one in 1969, a hit he never really matched; Trios 1982 smash Da Da Da; Phyllis Nelsons 1985 lovers rock-style classic Move Closer; and the New Radicals 1999 hit You Get What You Give. We travel through the years selecting some of your favourite number ones and a few others that came close, revealing whats happened to the one-hit wonders since.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: A look into the BBCs music archives for a reminder of one-hit wonders by the Las, the Mock Turtles, Joan Jett and Kim Carnes. There are also some surprising names on the list: Don Henley, Patti Smith, Curtis Mayfield and John Denver.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some indelible hits by artists we hardly heard from again. Featuring Peter Sarstedts Where Do You Go To My Lovely? a number one in 1969, a hit he never really matched; Trios 1982 smash Da Da Da; Phyllis Nelsons 1985 lovers rock-style classic Move Closer; and the New Radicals 1999 hit You Get What You Give. We travel through the years selecting some of your favourite number ones and a few others that came close, revealing whats happened to the one-hit wonders since.

programme.title: Sound of Metal
programme.desc: A heavy-metal drummers life is thrown into freefall when he begins to lose his hearing.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Tender dominates the headlines, sending shockwaves across the media and the government.

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Clive is seething when a truckload of steaming horse manure is dumped in front of Michaels house. Kacey comes round to help bury the jars and asks Michael about the hoarded boxes.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Kensukes Kingdom
programme.desc: Young Michael and his dog wash up on a remote island when a violent storm sweeps them overboard during a sailing trip. Believing hes all alone, Michael is surprised to discover a mysterious Japanese man whos lived on the island since World War II.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: On the Eve of the Salmon Season - The River Tweed
programme.desc: With the new salmon-fishing season about to open on the River Tweed, Sammi Kinghorn and Matt Baker follow the final preparations before the first cast of the year.

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Ian and Major head to Fort William, the biggest town in the Highlands, and the UKs self-styled outdoor capital.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent Scotland. Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: More delicious recipes from Richard Bainbridge, Julie Lin, Gaz Oakley and Shauna Froydenlund! Also, Rick Stein and Clive Myrie head out across the UK and abroad.

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp
programme.desc: A British epic about the life and loves of a reactionary army officer, Clive Candy VC, told episodically in flashback. From his dashing career in the Boer War of 1902, the film follows Candy through marriage during the First World War to the Blitz of 1943, when he searches London for his lost youth and loves.

programme.title: Angels One Five
programme.desc: A Hurricane pilot falls victim to the wrath of his squadron after a crash-landing, and must battle to win favour, as well as battling the Luftwaffe.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is at Huddersfield Town Hall in West Yorkshire. Experts Nick Davies and Christina Trevanion search for antiques to take to auction, and Paul delves into the areas coal mining history at the National Coal Mining Museum for England.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Ally and son Noah apply the salon treatment to go head-to-head with mainland breeders.

programme.title: Mens Six Nations
programme.desc: All the talking points and in-depth analysis from Round 4 of the Six Nations. England face a tricky trip to Rome against Italy, Ireland host Wales in Dublin and Scotland welcome France to Murrayfield.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor in Ealing, west London, where finds include a quirky 18th-century Harlequinade childrens flip book, a vintage trainer collection and a flying helmet from the Battle of Britain. Alastair Chandler comes across a unique pocket watch that symbolises a familys story on the eve of the Second World War, and Jon Baddeley is enthralled by the story behind a medal awarded to a suffragette.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: With the dominant males gone, trouble looms as intruder lions close in on the Xudum prides young cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her missing cub after a tense encounter, and Pobe the cheetah must defend her familys food from bigger cats.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Multiple cameras follow serious crime investigations in real time, revealing the crucial role cutting edge forensic science now plays in bringing criminals to justice.

programme.title: Platoon
programme.desc: A vivid and harrowing depiction of war which follows a 19-year-old college dropout who signs up for service in Vietnam in September 1967, and finds himself plunged into the bloody chaos of life as a GI on the front line. The winner of four Academy Awards, including Best Director and Best Picture, this is an uncompromising and semi-autobiographical study of men at war from writer-director Oliver Stone.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Harriet and Kit investigate the apparent suicide of a British-Chinese activist and must navigate complex international tensions.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Features a studio audience quizzing top politicians on the events of the day. The panel surrounding presenter Fiona Bruce is composed of at least one member of the three major parties.

programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent Scotland. Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: Swamp People
programme.desc: Series following charismatic alligator hunters on Americas largest swamp.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: King of the Swamp Troy Landry teaches long time alligator hunting partner Pickle Wheat the ins and outs of python hunting, but sometimes even the most experienced hunters walk away with scars.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Golds set up a sting to try to catch a thief and a customer brings in a vintage police car.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and a team of antiques dealers visit Ilford, to give locals a chance to put their valuables into auction or accept a cash sum. Items brought along include a daguerreotype brooch, a tea caddy and a diamond and gold watch.

programme.title: Unsealed: Alien Files
programme.desc: An investigation into compelling UFO and alien cases, opening previously off-limits secret files. The mysterious Blue Planet Project could hold the all the answers.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Aliens may have tried to make contact with Earthlings for millennia, and a message may have been decoded.

programme.title: Unsealed: Alien Files
programme.desc: Experts investigate why so many UFO sightings take place in Los Angeles, determining that the answer may lie underwater.

programme.title: Unsealed: Alien Files
programme.desc: Could historys most powerful men know more about extra-terrestrials than us? Experts explore the complex relationship between presidents and aliens.

programme.title: Unsealed: Alien Files
programme.desc: NASA denies UFO accounts and takes measures to prevent astronauts from telling their stories.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Witnesses, abductions and mass sightings have not been considered credible enough. Finding physical evidence will be a very big step toward full disclosure.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Meeting people who experienced alien life up close and examining three types of close encounters: sightings, fleeting interactions and abductions.

programme.title: UFO: POV
programme.desc: The Federal Aviation Administration refuses to investigate after 12 United Airlines employees report seeing a grey disc-shaped object hovering above Gate C17 at the Chicago OHare International Airport, leading some to think it is a cover-up.

programme.title: Encounter: UFO
programme.desc: They keep coming back. There are areas around the world that seem to keep attracting UFOs, where objects return to the skies again and again, as if taunting witnesses below.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Monster in the Attic, Houston UFO and Utah Bigfoot
programme.desc: Is a video showing a strange formation of flickering lights the latest proof that Houston is a UFO hotspot? What is this mysterious humanoid creature caught on camera in an abandoned attic?

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Chupacabra, Greatest UFO Photo Ever and Mars Sounds
programme.desc: Whats the truth behind a recent caught on camera sighting of Chupacabra outside of an Amarillo Zoo? Is an aerial photograph taken over Costa Rica the greatest UFO picture ever taken? Are strange sounds recorded on Mars a message from an alien race?

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: When satellites spot a mysterious structure in North Korea, experts investigate if this sinister evidence points to a looming nuclear threat, and using cutting-edge tech, they reveal dark secrets from this strange place.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: When a strange pattern appears in a field, experts wonder if its an extraterrestrial response to a message sent to an alien star cluster in 1974, and using cutting-edge science, they uncover its true origin.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: When a sinister cave is discovered in a remote corner of Iceland, experts uncover a secret underworld tunneling to unknown depths, and using cutting-edge tech, they investigate if this strange place could hold secrets of alien worlds.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In 1997, thousands of people across Phoenix witness a series of strange lights unlike anything theyve ever seen. Could this be one of the largest-scale UFO sightings in history, or is there another explanation for this mysterious phenomenon?

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: One of the holiest and most powerful relics from the Bible has been missing for over 2,000 years. While some believe the Ark of the Covenant was destroyed, many people think its still out there. Could new clues finally tell us what happened?

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In 1930, a New York Supreme Court justice with close ties to Tammany Hall, and future president Franklin Delano Roosevelt, steps out of a steakhouse and disappears. For decades, the case dominates mainstream media and remains unsolved.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: Legendary beasts are believed to roam the earth and the seas, terrorising, killing, and even possessing humans. But are these creatures merely metaphors or is there proof theyve actually been unleashed to wreak havoc?

programme.title: Clash of Ancient Warriors
programme.desc: Battle of Sekigahara: The Battle of Sekigahara saw Tokugawa Ieyasu emerge victorious over his rival Ishida Mitsunari, uniting the country under the Tokugawa shogunate.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Many mysteries of planet Earth are far from understood. How was Devils Tower formed? Or why do some people get struck by lightning multiple times?

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Millions of people around the world claim to have had an encounter with creatures unlike any recognised by mainstream science, such as the Mothman, Chupacabra, and Mongolian Death Worm.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Lost Language, The Beauty Machine And The Disk Of Strangeness
programme.desc: Examine a tablet linked to a lost civilization, a machine that measures beauty, and an ancient Egyptian object.

programme.desc: NASA denies UFO accounts and takes measures to prevent astronauts from telling their stories.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.sub-title: Do You Know Who I Am?
programme.desc: An impatient SUV driver in Oregon attacks a cyclist at a red light and gets serious comeuppance; a congressional candidate threatens an officers job when she pulls him over for speeding and texting while driving.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: A distracted police officer in Missouri strikes a bicyclist with his vehicle; a woman is caught red-handed keying another persons car in Louisiana; a motorcyclist crashes into a ditch after being sideswiped by a jeep in Illinois.

programme.title: Rust Valley Restorers
programme.desc: JF begins restoring a 1928 Model A Roadster, while Avery takes on a 1941 military vehicle and these antique machines are full of challenges. Mike chases redemption at the races.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly: Henrys Wreck-Trospective
programme.desc: ...Henrys Wreck-trospective. The Bromley Wreck-Trospective: Get ready for a high-octane nostalgia trip as Junk and Disorderly Henrys Wreck-trospective dives into the archives.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly: Henrys Wreck-Trospective
programme.desc: Motoring maverick Henry Cole goes through over a decades worth of footage, featuring oily chaos, revisiting the best barn finds, examining the worst breakdowns and biggest rebuilds.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other peoples automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The team find a Rolls-Royce that lures them to Yorkshire. Although it is old, the car is very economical, and the high quality can still be felt.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Chum kick it with a rare pair of Pumas from 1968. Next, Chum is blown away by a fan from the 1940s. Later, Rick reads into a collection of letters from historical figures, but its some lab notes by Marie Curie that really leave him glowing.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Corey has an appetite for a deal when a Guns N Roses collector box rocks into the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Corey becomes enchanted with a collection of magical memorabilia; rare first edition copy of "Anne of Green Gables"; a vintage Harley t-shirt makes Corey go hog wild; Rick and Chum head to California to take a peek at a massive Disney collection.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: The guys leave an auction empty handed and try to find new merchandise at a yard sale, and questionable antique tennis rackets and an ancient special effects box may not earn them anything.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: The shops landlord wants to talk to the guys, and Allen puts an experimental powered parachute to the test.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: A ruthless store owner tries to stop Ton and Allen from scoring new items in Santa Barbara so they fight back, and the guys uncover a rare antique air gun and a vintage wooden boat built for speed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: When their truck breaks down, Ton and Allen lay it all on the line to raise money to fix her. They score a Foosball table, Tommy Gun drums and to Tons delight, a treasure trove of Twinkies.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Rene goes big; Ivy scores a packed locker and stumbles across an antique of unusual origin; Darrell grabs a smaller unit but finds that good things do, indeed, come in petite packages sometimes.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Stanton in the Place Where You Work
programme.desc: Rene gets lukewarm results bobbing for profits in Stanton. Dusty finds a hoard of quality equipment, inking himself a decent payday. Looking for treasure, Brandi ends up with the green in an unexpected manner.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry believes all the whales are coming to the big auction in Fullerton as he takes Dusty under his wing to test him. Rene makes some dainty discoveries, and Darrells tool locker becomes quite eye-opening.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The women outnumber the men in Rosamond, as Dusty and his girlfriend discover a stockpile of vintage TV memorabilia; Brandi enlists the help of both Danielle and Lisa to dig for gold.

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: As winter descends on Oak Island and clues continue to be uncovered, the fellowship makes a final bold effort to find the treasure before operations cease for the winter.

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: The 2021 Himalayan floods in Uttarakhand, India, sparked fears of buried nuclear weapons. Scientists discovered that the disaster was caused by a collapsing glacier, revealing the environmental impact of ancient geopolitical tensions.

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A recent discovery in the Sierra Nevada brings the tragic story of the Donner Party back to life. Among the artifacts preserved in the ice since 1846 are shards of glass and a writing slate.

programme.desc: The Danger Agency realises that a lack of accounting has left them bankrupt. They find out that the wind makes waves on the sea and that it can help boats and birds to travel more quickly. On the way, they spot a washing line with clothes drying in the wind and blow some bubbles to show how the wind helps seeds to grow in new places.

programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet some colourful characters - the amazing mandrills, which are the largest species of monkey in the world. They get close enough to see the beautiful blue and red faces of the males. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet sand lizards. They are surrounded by many females, who blend in perfectly with their sandy home, as well as some colourful males, who are much easier to spot.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Aussie-born Adam is taking a risk planning an 80s Aussie soap wedding when bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Celebrating the freaky, frenetic and funny side of family life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell and Manny find out theyve got more in common than they thought, while Cameron and Gloria spend some quality time together.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Its Phils birthday and he desperately wants the years must-have gadget. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to find his inner mean machine.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Jay take the reigns when Luke and Mannys hot-tempered basketball coach quits during a game. Meanwhile, Mitchell invites Cameron to meet a prospective employer.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon tries to turn around the ailing fortunes of Grasshopper Also, a struggling restaurant in Carlstadt, New Jersey.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon tries to turn around the ailing fortunes of an Italian restaurant in Boston, which is run by two brothers.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Bridgeport, Connecticut to visit Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that is more than just a culinary disaster. Run by a husband and wife team, the restaurant is being neglected as they teeter on the edge of divorce.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon tries to turn around the ailing fortunes of Zekes, a New Orleans restaurant serving soul food to the loving locals.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A believer in the mantra finders keepers, a sticky-fingered Bart steals actor Denis Learys phone and uses it for pranking purposes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Homer and Lisa Exchange Cross Words
programme.desc: Lisa discovers she has an impressive knack for solving crossword puzzles and she enters in a city-wide crossword tournament. Feelings are hurt though when Homer bets against Lisa in the championship match.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer suspects Barts new friend Bashir of involvement with a terrorist plot to destroy the Springfield Mall.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wonders if fate truly did deal him a tough hand after he finds out that the class presidential election he lost in high school was rigged.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Barney are recruited by NASA to make it more appealing to the man in the street, and when Barney falls off the wagon, Homer is chosen to go into space.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer decides to become Flanders new best friend after attending a football game with him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart wins a radio quiz, he can choose between a large cash prize and an elephant. Bart christens his new elephant and best friend Stampy, but Homer cant afford his upkeep and decides to sell him to an ivory dealer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr Burns decides he wants an heir and auditions Springfields children for the position. When Bart wins, he must choose between his boring family and the rich, over-indulgent Mr Burns.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Principal Skinner is fired as a result of Barts pranks and Flanders is hired as his replacement.

programme.title: The Greatest Showman
programme.desc: P.T. Barnum indulges his limitless imagination to create the Barnum and Bailey circus in the 1800s. Featuring musical numbers, exotic performers and daring feats, the mesmerizing spectacle takes the world by storm to become the greatest show on Earth.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo, Mel B, Clare Balding, Kadeena Cox, Mo Gilligan and Ellie Simmonds are some of the many household names hitting the sofa for this weeks best TV.

programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Dejà Vu
programme.desc: Dejà vu is what unexpectedly guides agent Doug Carlin through an investigation into a crime. After an explosion in New Orleans, Doug is called to check and recover the evidence, but Carlins enquiries uncover a connection between the bomb accelerant and the body of a local girl named Claire Kuchever. Things become even stranger when Doug hears his own voice on Claires answering machine.

programme.title: Undergods
programme.desc: An otherworldly journey through a Europe in decline, as a collection of ill-fated characters confront their doomed futures and fortunes.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Admirable Crichton
programme.desc: A group of shipwrecked English aristocrats stake their survival on the ingenuity of their trustworthy butler.

programme.title: The Lavender Hill Mob
programme.desc: Henry Holland is a fussy supervisor who oversees gold bullion deliveries to the bank in which he works. Secretly, he is plotting to steal a load of bullion and retire early, but he cannot figure out a way to smuggle it out of the country.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: Ever since her parents left her as a baby, little Annie has led a hard-knock life with her mean foster-mother, Miss Hannigan. However, that all changes when hard-nosed billionaire and mayor candidate Will Stacks takes her in on the recommendation of his advisers. Stacks believes that hes Annies guardian angel, but the plucky youngsters confidence and sunny outlook may mean that Annie will save Will instead.

programme.title: The Day the Earth Stood Still
programme.desc: A flying saucer piloted by alien Klaatu, lands in Washington to warn Earth to change its warring ways or risk destruction. Along the way Klaatu finds time to learn more about Earth in the company of Helen Benson and her son.

programme.title: Central Intelligence
programme.desc: A lethal CIA agent and his former classmate encounter shootouts, espionage and double-crosses while trying to save the U.S. spy satellite system.

programme.title: The Menu
programme.desc: Shocking surprises await a couple when they travel to a coastal island to sample a chefs lavish menu at an exclusive restaurant.

programme.title: Orphan: First Kill
programme.desc: After escaping from a psychiatric facility in Estonia, Esther travels to America by impersonating the missing daughter of a wealthy family. Yet, an unexpected twist arises that pits her against a mother who will protect her family at any cost.

programme.title: Miss Peregrines Home for Peculiar Children
programme.desc: When teenager Jacob travels to a mystical island he discovers some crumbling ruins. As he explores the rooms and hallways he learns that its previous residents possessed incredible powers, and that they had some very scary enemies.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys going off the beaten path to check out funky finds. In North Carolina, an eatery tucked inside a beer store slings crazy waffle creations, and in Texas, a not-your-everyday fusion joint mashes up barbecue with sushi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into the traditions of many different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heats up with spice and cools off with ice starting with hot dishes in California and Virginia before chilling with a Hawaiian tropical shaved ice.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a multi-generational joint bringing NOLA to LA, a unique Minneapolis deli serving up meatless `meats and a Hawaiian noodle spot dishing out ramen and Vietnamese crepes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Lunch with Ina is up for auction. The winning bidders can look forward to a delicious lunch of chilled cucumber soup with shrimp, lobster and shells salad, along with Eton Mess.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its Friday night and Ina is cooking up a delicious steak dinner for Jeffrey. This time Jeffreys making his own supper on the grill. Theres herbed ricotta bruschetta, grilled steak and Arugula.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is taking a trip down memory lane and updating classic recipes with a modern take. First up theres meatloaf, then shes updating an appetiser classic - smoked salmon devilled eggs.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Today is all about desserts in Inas hall of fame. She makes an incredible frozen mocha mousse, easy French apple galettes and balsamic strawberries.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is sharing her secrets on how to get professional results at home. Theres a Barefoot classic - lemon chicken breasts - and a recipe from one of her favourite New York restaurants.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is all about easy desserts. Theres a fruit filled cranberry and apple cake and a dessert master class from patisserie chef, Claudia Fleming, who shares her recipe for lemon cream napoleon.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to heart, baking treats to share with loved ones. She kicks things off with her epic chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Baking spectacular treats can be pricey, and after a strained 2020, many of us are looking for ways to tighten the purse strings. Nadiya believes you can create something delicious without breaking the bank or compromising on flavour.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite biscuits and bites to snack on at any time of the day. Nadiya believes that a homemade biscuit is one of lifes greatest pleasures, so she kicks off with her simple but extremely beautiful amaretti biscuits.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya takes things up a baking gear as she turns her attention to celebration bakes. Having won The Great British Bake Off and baked the Queens 90th birthday cake, Nadiya is a true expert when it comes to elaborate cakes to mark a special occasion.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Jamess culinary tour takes him to the Camargue, where he learns all about the Camargue bull and the delicate hand-harvested salty crust fleur de sel.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James is in the city of Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and home to the largest market in Provence. James cooks a dish of John Dory using produce from the market.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Bikers cook dishes perfect for cosy suppers, comfort straight from the oven, some for sharing everyday, others for a special treat. Theres a new take on classic steak and chips, and a sticky gingerbread thats got to be the ultimate in comfort baking.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave cook dishes guaranteed to turn up the heat. They have a spicy new take on a family favourite with their roasted spiced cauliflower cheese, and they take inspiration from the Caribbean with a plate of jerk pork chops.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Dave enjoys a taste of his native county, Cumbria, as the Bikers cook recipes with great local ingredients. These include a comforting supper dish made from Cumberland sausage and the ultimate Cumbrian dessert, sticky toffee pudding.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers cook comfort food oozing with traditional values. Theres a twist on a classic pudding, a perfect teatime treat, and Si and Dave take two breakfast favourites to make one lip-smacking new dish.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam Richman seeks out off-menu dishes in Reykjavik, Iceland, including a holiday-inspired pizza with unusual toppings, a meatball sandwich at a laundromat and traditional Icelandic delicacies hidden in a bus station.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam Richman looks for hidden eats in Ilulissat and Oqaatsut, Greenland, including off-menu reindeer schnitzel, fried rice that combines Thai and Greenlandic flavours and an off-the-grid restaurant he must pass icebergs to find.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris grabbing creative flavour combos that pack a punch! First, an NYC couple is bringing Greek grub to Naples, FL. A Dallas spot is serving over-the-top pork belly lollipops, and a Triple-G winner is putting out next-level ramen in Chicago.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris diving into Kenyan curried goat and rocking Moroccan lamb in Tallahassee, FL. Plus, a Chicago spot is serving up big-time bechamel arancini, and a deli in Gainesville, FL, is bringing the heat with salt-and-pepper smoky sausages.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits the road looking for the best chicken dinners and finds a rocker mums favourite chicken, fried Thai wings and a California hot dog spot with unique chicken and waffles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits up three American cities for big international flavours. He visits a Filipino joint in LA, samples Ethiopian cuisine in San Francisco and bites into Indian classics in Minneapolis.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys getting ready for Turkey Day! In Princeton, New Jersey, Reverend Run jumps in for crepes, a New Hampshire cafe cooks up terrific turkey meatloaf and Guy finds the spirit of the season in Louisville at a `pay-what-you-can joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is checking out fried bologna and veggie burgers in Louisville, KY, and brunch staples at a diner-restaurant mash up in Portland, Oregon.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys picking up burgers to buns to bagels. In Portsmouth, NH, he visits a deli serving lamb burgers and ribs, and in Los Angeles, a Koreatown favourite.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys going off the beaten path to check out funky finds. In North Carolina, an eatery tucked inside a beer store slings crazy waffle creations, and in Texas, a not-your-everyday fusion joint mashes up barbecue with sushi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heads to Cancun, Mexico, to dig into cuisine at places like an authentic, family-owned spot where Grandma is cooking up rellenos and killer chicharron gorditas. Then, get an inside look at Guys Playa del Carmen restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits an all-female lineup thats rockin the house in Houston and Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into the traditions of many different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heats up with spice and cools off with ice starting with hot dishes in California and Virginia before chilling with a Hawaiian tropical shaved ice.

programme.desc: Guy digs into a righteous mashup of flavours across the country, including a potato-centric palace dishing out hearty Brazilian specialties and a dynamite deli serving up real-deal latkes and turkey pastrami.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri hunts down desert treasure and island flavour, starting with a breakfast-lunch cafe in California. Then he heads to a funky gluten-free spot in Hawaii, and a crazy Caribbean joint in Virginia.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: From wild boar gnocchi in Los Angeles to a lamb belly BLT in Miami, Guys grabbing grub cooked up in pubs across the US.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. Adam attempts the Ultimate Destroyer Challenge at Papa Bobs Bar-B-Que in Kansas City.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. He attempts the Ludicrous Wings challenge: that means guzzling 12 super spicy hot wings in fifteen minutes or less.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The ever-hungry Adam Richman chomps his way across America In Portland, he attempts the Manimal Challenge in which he must eat a 4lb meal in 20 minutes or less.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman chomps his way across America. He takes on the Jumbolie Challenge, attempting to eat a five pound Stromboli in less than an hour.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Des Moines, Adam takes on the Adam Emmenecker Challenge at Jethros BBQ: 15 minutes to finish a mammoth sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. This time, he attempts the Big Ugly Burger Challenge at Bubs Burger Ice Cream in Indianapolis.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. In Syracuse, he takes on the Frittata Challenge: a breakfast mountain weighing in at over 6-lbs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way across America. He heads to Niagara Falls for an Italian food challenge involving 10 plates and 7lb worth of food in 90 minutes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Jamess journey brings him to the adopted home of his hero Keith Floyd - the pretty riverside town of LIsle sur la Sorgue, where Floyd once had a restaurant.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Jamess journey takes him to Pegasus Bridge - famous for the landings on D-Day. Normandy is full of orchards and dairy farms - because it is cider and calvados country.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Bread Week, and the remaining bakers have a lot to prove in a sweet and savoury signature, a technical that honours the NHS, and an ambitious, artistic showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Chocolate Week and the bakers tackle a deceptively simple traybake signature, a technical packed full of chocolate and nuts, and a white chocolate celebration showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Pastry Week and the bakers put their stamp on a Cornish classic in the signature, go retro in the technical, and make a showstopping classic tart hidden within a cage.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its a Bake Off first - Japanese Week - and the bakers tackle steamed buns, a layered technical and a showstopper inspired by kawaii, the Japanese love of all things cute.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers roar back into life with a madcap tour of the Mississippi. They visit Memphis for the tastes and sounds of soul, meeting the queen of soul food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, who will help decide which chef will go forward to the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the south wests finest chefs - Jude Kereama, Josh Eggleton and Dominic Chapman - attempt to impress a veteran judge with their starters. As the only newcomer in the kitchen, Jude is determined to plate up a winning dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three south west chefs fight it out for the fish course. Despite schoolboy errors during the starter, experienced Dominic is determined to get a high score on the course. But will newcomer Jude, a seafood master, overcome his nerves to plate up?.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course as the three south-west chefs go head to head. Newcomer Jude is brimming with determination and confidence as the main course battle hits the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The south-west chefs have one last chance to impress as they face the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges; with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining south-west judges must cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Joining the judges is Radhika Bynon, a member of the Forest Gate Womens Institute, a modern urban group of women.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael are challenged to plate up perfect starter courses for this weeks veteran judge. Its the first time in the Great British Menu kitchen for all three chefs, so who will triumph and who will fall?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael fight it out for the fish course. Experimental chef Stephen is determined to prove himself with a fish course honouring the WIs tradition of preserving, but hes up against stiff competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with the Welsh chefs going head to head. With all three chefs paying homage to Welsh lamb; Adam with a refined faggot, Phil with a dish dedicated to his Mum and Stephen with a lamb dish celebrating the WI.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the tricky dessert course and the last chance for the Welsh chefs to secure their place in tomorrows regional final. With everything to play for, the pressure is fierce. Only two can make it through, and one will leave the competition for good.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two remaining Welsh chefs cook their four courses again for the judges and Rosemary Bishton. One chef will be chosen to represent Wales in the National Finals taking them a step closer to cooking at the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Northern Irelands top chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold - go head to head with their starter courses to impress this weeks veteran judge. Former champion Chris is back for glory and determined to reach the banquet, but hes up against two newcomers with their sights firmly set on victory.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold are cooking their fish courses. Ambitious Ben is determined to steal Chriss regional crown, but under fierce scrutiny from this weeks formidable veteran, will the pressure to deliver spell melt down?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of Northern Irelands finest chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold. Ambitious Ben is out to prove himself with a perfect lamb main course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Northern Irish chefs are all striving for one of the two spots in the Regional Final. The pressure is intense, and in the end one chef must leave the competition empty handed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North Irish chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - award winning food writer Felicity Cloake.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen are going head to head this week for a chance to represent the North East region in the National Finals. Today its a battle for starter course, and the first chance to impress their formidable veteran chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fight for the fish course, as three of the North Easts finest chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - are out to impress. After his risky starter caused a stir yesterday Michael is planning another controversial dish in honour of the WIs support of womens rights.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the North Easts most dynamic chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - go head to head cooking their main courses. Michelin starred Tim is super keen to bag the first ten of the week, but Michael and Mini are hot on his heels.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with the dessert course. Its been a week of highs and lows, modern audacious dishes and classic masterpieces but with only two places up for grabs cooking for the judges, at the end of today who will leave the competition empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - WI president Kirsty Bowen from Sheffield - who will help decide which chef will go through to represent the North East in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Representing the North West are Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and returning contender Mark Ellis. Todays the starter course, and after failing to cook for the judges last year Marks out to redeem himself but he faces stiff competition in executive chef Eve and young gun Matt.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course, as three of the North Wests finest chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis - go head to head. Eve and Matt are hoping their classic approach to cooking fish will win the day whilst Mark is going all out with fishy take on a traditional dessert recipe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the main course for the three North West chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis. Mark remains determined and focused on his battle to get to judges chamber this year, but as the pressure to plate up perfection reaches fever pitch, will all three chefs get a dish to the pass?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North West chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow. Its the end of the road for one chef, but who will it be?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by award winning food writer Sabrina Ghayour and together theyll decide which chef will go on to represent the North West in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course for three of London and the South Easts hottest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt Gillan is determined to make it to this years banquet, but he faces fierce competition in the two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund - go head to head with their fish courses. Lee is hoping his modern plate of mackerel and passion fruit will outclass Matt and Marks more traditionally inspired salmon dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of London and the South Easts finest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt is determined to bring home a 10 with his ambitious celebration of the billy goat but has he bitten off more than he can chew?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the London and South East chefs are striving to secure one of the two spots in tomorrows Regional Final. The scores are close, so its all to play for, as today marks the end of the competition for one man.

programme.title: Wartime Crime

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.desc: The soldiers surrounded in Stalingrad ran short of everything, from food and medical supplies to ultimately even hope itself.

programme.title: Survivors of Stalingrad: The Russian-German War

programme.title: Total War

programme.title: Total War

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how people and resources became absorbed in the war efforts by occupying forces during WWII.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: The cloak of war played a role in the genocide of WWII, allowing unfathomable horrors against innocent women, children, and men. Even within Allied nations, individuals were seen as a potential enemies and denied liberty.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Bundles of tree trunks are smashed apart, releasing thousands of logs on the shoreline. Jeremy and his crew must work fast to recover the cargo.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray pairs up with local comedians to explore the history of British colonial rule and its legacy. They find out about the origins of British rule, its impact and how its legacy continues to influence governance, culture, and attitudes.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The team find evidence from one of Scotlands bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the Scottish Highlands, the site of the first major clash between Highland Jacobites and the government army.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores British history through the stories of individual towns and examines Britain in wartime by looking at the coastal town of Dover.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britains most Roman town.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In York, Professor Alice Roberts takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds, and gets her hands on Viking poo.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britains most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UKs most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canarys origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores British history through the stories of individual towns and examines Britain in wartime by looking at the coastal town of Dover.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the worlds first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Georgian Edinburghs finest architecture, the origins of `Auld Reekie, the roots of modern Scottishness, and some dark imperial controversies.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the English Civil War through the history of Oxford, the city at the heart of a battle between an autocratic king and his rebellious parliament.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the citys story.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in London, as she attempts to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. She learns the violent and vengeful truth behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelsons dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and the lives of sex workers.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Who the (Bleep) Did I Marry?
programme.desc: Single mother Brooklin meets a gorgeous guy online; despite many red flags, she marries him and is soon alienated from family and close to financial ruin.

programme.title: Love, Honor, Betray
programme.desc: Jeanne and Stephen Trantel have the dream life. But when Stephen loses his job after 9/11, their relationship splinters, and Jeanne suspects he is hiding a terrible secret - until an unexpected call reveals the shocking truth.

programme.title: Seduced to Slay
programme.desc: When single mother Linda Damm goes missing in Lafayette, Colorado, police get a tip from an anonymous caller telling them where to find her body and who her unexpected killer is. Is there more to the case than meets the eye?

programme.title: Seduced to Slay
programme.desc: Thirty miles outside of Dallas, in Royse City, Texas, Fire Captain Robert Poynter and his young wife Chacey enjoy an idyllic small-town life. But thats shattered when a dramatic physical transformation leads to infidelity and a dead body.

programme.title: Where Murder Lies
programme.desc: A couple learns their spicy lifestyle may not be a secret to everyone.

programme.title: Where Murder Lies
programme.desc: A Texas housewife finds it easier to keep a secret than to live with one.

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: In 2013, police arrive at a devastating scene. At first, it appears to be a tragic accident, but police soon uncover a story of infidelity and obsession.

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: A married couple are found shot dead in their home. Investigators reveal a bizarre story that involves drugs, stolen money and a pet parrot who is becomes a witness.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.
